titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Elizabeth Tyrrell, 2 August 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: William Upton Tyrrell is experiencing heavy rain in the trenches.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
2 August 17 My dearest Mamy No news at all but just a
line as usual. It is still pouring
rain. It has been raining steadily
for 40 hours now. It is really too
bad that it should start at such a time. We have just got the papers now
and things seem to be going splendidly
but I am afraid they will be seriously
hampered by the wet. This is such
an awful country for mud when it
does rain. Also the harvest is just
coming in and now it is all getting
lodged. However it may clear up again &
then all will be well. I have not got any letters yet since
Sunday but will get them all in a
bunch very soon - probably today I hop you have been getting mine
alright. I wrote a long letter to Jasper
today as I had plenty of time. Sorry to hear Dr. Waters has
been so ill again. I wrote to
Mrs Waters also today. Put a piece
of soap in a parcel soon please. No news but best love to all
from
Your most affec Son
Willie
